notes: Non-discriminating: baseline read User.md and reproduced the protocol. 14 tool calls, 23.7k tokens — required more effort to find same answer. Eval 3 assertions need update.
Eval 1 (task3-clustering-setup) is the strongest discriminator: skill +1.00 vs baseline +0.20. Key misses in baseline: LaTeX cells, numpy boundary, cache pattern, retriever call.

Evals 2 and 3 are non-discriminating because the baseline agent reads User.md, which already names Node/Link/NetworkManager and documents the rollback protocol. Current assertions don't test skill-unique behavior.
Skill adds efficiency: with_skill responses use fewer tokens and fewer tool calls (mean 7.7 vs 12.3) because context is pre-loaded by the skill rather than browsed from scratch.

Recommended assertion additions for iteration 2: (Eval 2) 'NetworkManager includes numpy boundary methods like coords_array() or adjacency_matrix()'; (Eval 3) 'Response instructs adding assert BEFORE optimizer call as proactive gate, not just post-hoc rollback'.
High variance in with_skill time (44s stddev) is expected — Eval 3 is fast (50s, no retriever needed), Eval 1-2 involve retriever awk calls.
